name: Tom Yum Goong
method: Heat a little vegetable oil in a stainless-steel pan over medium-high heat, then sauté the shells and heads of the prawns, using a spatula to squeeze out any tomalley from the prawn head.
Heat a little vegetable oil in a stainless-steel pan over medium-high heat, then sauté the shells and heads of the prawns, using a spatula to squeeze out any tomalley from the prawn head.
Add onion and simmer on medium-low heat for 45 minutes. When done, strain the soup, making sure to remove any liquid from the prawn head.
Add onion and simmer on medium-low heat for 45 minutes. When done, strain the soup, making sure to remove any liquid from the prawn head.
Place lemon, kaffir lime leaves, galangal, and chilies into the soup (Step 3). Let it boil for 5 minutes.
Place lemon, kaffir lime leaves, galangal, and chilies into the soup (Step 3). Let it boil for 5 minutes.
Add the oyster mushrooms and let the soup boil again.
Add the oyster mushrooms and let the soup boil again.
Stir in lemon juice, fish sauce, chili paste and sugar.
Stir in lemon juice, fish sauce, chili paste and sugar.
